title: Object Types, Interfaces, Aliases, and Structural Compatibility · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: Design public service records that evolve without silently accepting misspelled, mutable, or incompatible fields. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an evolvable service-record contract library and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.
Choose interface or type alias from extension, unions, declaration merging, diagnostics, and API intent.
Predict structural assignability while identifying where extra members, methods, and variance create risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A scheduling record has id "job-2", an optional owner, readonly creation time, and a mutable status transition. Reject a misspelled status field, protect the invariant, and show which readonly promise exists only at compile time. Competency probe: Model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.

Inspect the Object Property Modifiers trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: An optional property is always identical to a required property whose value includes undefined.
